stories.headline: US blockade of Iranian ports explained in two minutes
stories.summary: The United States has implemented restrictions on Iranian ports, limiting Iran's ability to export oil and conduct maritime trade as part of broader sanctions enforcement. The blockade affects shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global chokepoint, while some sanctioned tankers continue to navigate the route despite the restrictions.

stories.headline: Americanswers... on 5 Live! Donald Trump vs Pope Leo on Iran
stories.summary: Trump and a papal figure named Leo have disagreed publicly over Iran policy, with the conflict touching on questions about religious authority's role in foreign affairs. The dispute reflects broader tensions between Trump's administration and Catholic leadership over the appropriate scope of papal commentary on U.S. political decisions.

stories.headline: Vance ‘sad Orbán lost’ but says US will work with new Magyar government in Hungary – Europe live
stories.summary: Hungary has elected a new prime minister, Péter Magyar, ending Viktor Orbán's long tenure as leader. U.S. Vice President Vance expressed disappointment over Orbán's loss but committed to working constructively with Hungary's incoming government.

stories.headline: Mark Carney secures majority government in Canada after special election win
stories.summary: Mark Carney's Liberal Party has won enough seats in a special election to form a majority government in Canada's Parliament. This result gives Carney's administration the ability to pass legislation without needing support from other parties.

stories.headline: Retrial over death of Argentina legend Maradona to begin
stories.summary: A retrial begins in Argentina regarding Diego Maradona's 2020 death, as authorities re-examine the circumstances surrounding the football legend's passing. The proceedings will determine whether medical professionals bear responsibility in connection with Maradona's death at age 60.

stories.headline: Man charged with attempted murder of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman
stories.summary: A man has been charged with attempted murder following a Molotov cocktail attack on OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's California home. The incident marks a serious escalation in violence directed at a prominent artificial intelligence industry figure.

stories.headline: Lebanon seeks peace, but Hezbollah needs to be convinced first
stories.summary: Lebanese and Israeli officials are meeting directly in Washington for the first time in decades, with the United States facilitating discussions aimed at resolving border tensions and the broader conflict. Hezbollah's position remains uncertain, as the militant group's support will be crucial for any agreement to hold, even though Lebanon's government is pursuing negotiations.

stories.headline: Former Nato chief warns UK security 'in peril' as he accuses Starmer of 'corrosive complacency'
stories.summary: A former NATO chief has criticized the UK government's approach to defense spending and military preparedness, arguing that insufficient investment threatens national security. The critique reflects ongoing debate among security experts about whether current defense budgets and policies adequately address emerging threats to Britain.

stories.headline: Pope Leo visits Algeria in sign of Africa’s growing importance to Catholic church
stories.summary: Pope Leo's visit to Algeria represents the Catholic Church's strategic focus on Africa, where the faith is experiencing faster growth than in Europe and North America. The trip carries symbolic weight given Augustine's Algerian origins and the Pope's emphasis on reconciliation between communities historically divided by colonialism and conflict.

stories.headline: Spanish prime minister’s wife charged with corruption
stories.summary: Spain's Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez's wife faces corruption charges following a multi-year investigation into her business activities. The case marks a significant legal challenge for the sitting government and raises questions about transparency in Spain's political leadership.
